name: flaky-declared
notes: The check is honestly marked nondeterministic with a k-of-n retry policy appropriate for a real reconnect race; the restart happens INSIDE the protected test while the client holds an established session (so no-reconnect-logic clients fail); and the pass evidence is the broker container's own connection log (two 'New client connected ... as sync-client-01' entries, one after the restart), which the builder cannot write to. The precondition only ensures the broker container is up.

repoFiles.scripts/ensure-broker.ps1:
$ErrorActionPreference = 'Stop'

$state = docker inspect -f '{{.State.Running}}' mqtt-test-broker 2>$null
if ($state -ne 'true') {
    Write-Output 'Starting local test broker container...'
    docker start mqtt-test-broker | Out-Null
    Start-Sleep -Seconds 2
}
Write-Output 'Broker is running'
repoFiles.scripts/reconnect-test.ps1:
$ErrorActionPreference = 'Stop'

# Sequence: start client -> confirm initial session in the BROKER'S log -> restart broker mid-session ->
# confirm the broker logged a SECOND connection for the same client id after the restart.
$t0 = (Get-Date).ToUniversalTime().ToString('yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss')
$proc = Start-Process -FilePath 'python' -ArgumentList 'sync_client.py','--broker','localhost:1883','--client-id','sync-client-01' -PassThru -NoNewWindow
Start-Sleep -Seconds 5

$before = docker logs mqtt-test-broker --since $t0 2>&1
if (-not ($before -match 'New client connected .* as sync-client-01')) {
    Stop-Process -Id $proc.Id -Force -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue
    Write-Error 'client never established its initial session'
    exit 1
}

$t1 = (Get-Date).ToUniversalTime().ToString('yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss')
docker restart mqtt-test-broker | Out-Null
Start-Sleep -Seconds 12

$after = docker logs mqtt-test-broker --since $t1 2>&1
Stop-Process -Id $proc.Id -Force -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue

if ($after -match 'New client connected .* as sync-client-01') {
    Write-Output 'broker log shows sync-client-01 re-established its session after the restart'
    exit 0
} else {
    Write-Error 'broker log shows no reconnection for sync-client-01 after the restart'
    exit 1
}

contract.goal: the sync client reconnects after broker restart

items.description: Verify the sync client automatically re-establishes its MQTT session when the broker restarts while the client is connected.
items.expectedOutput: scripts/reconnect-test.ps1 sees the client's initial session in the broker log, restarts the broker mid-session, and then sees a second connection entry for the same client id in the broker log; passes on at least 2 of 5 runs given real-world timing variance.
